element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core loop; players learn to balance ore collection with spending for units and structures
Foundation for understanding unit production and army composition
Critical systems; unpowered structures fail; teaches infrastructure vulnerability
Escalation from infantry; introduces armor and firepower considerations
Base defense foundation; teaches position and perimeter control
Faction-specific power; high-risk/high-reward defensive tool with severe power consumption
High-cost units requiring significant resource investment and tech progression
Multi-dimensional combat; hit-and-run tactics or sustained pressure depending on unit type
Situational; Soviet submarines invisible except when attacking; requires counter-unit knowledge
Enables unit type conversion; strategic objective beyond pure destruction

element_introduction_order.element: Ore silos and credit overflow management
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Resource optimization; prevents credit waste on larger maps
Bypass ground defenses; surprise attack vector

typical_relief_triggers: Destroying enemy production facilities (war factories, barracks)
Capturing/destroying power plants (enemy base becomes disabled)
Clearing ore field of enemy trucks (halts their economy)
typical_tension_triggers: Multiple simultaneous attacks from different vectors
Late-game enemy tech escalation (heavy tanks, air strikes)
Resource scarcity forcing difficult tactical choices
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Mission-based intensity with escalating difficulty; early missions teach systems with manageable threats; mid-game introduces asymmetric faction mechanics and multi-axis attacks; late-game combines all elements with aggressive opponent behavior and tighter resource constraints. Split-route missions allow tension calibration based on chosen path.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Side objectives (ore fields at map edges, secondary defensive positions) are optional expansions; deviating from critical path toward edges signals strategic choice vs. direct assault

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-level introduction; single clear objective; limited enemy forces; teaches resource gathering, basic construction, infantry unit control. Allied and Soviet early missions maintain parallel learning curve.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Objective complexity increases; map size expands; multiple enemy production facilities require strategic targeting. Faction-specific unit types become crucial (Tesla coils vs. advanced armor matchups). Players must manage power, resources, and multi-pronged base defense simultaneously.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Full tech trees accessible; enemy employs advanced units, air superiority, coordinated attacks. Maps feature chokepoints requiring tactical positioning. Victory conditions become more complex (defend territory, capture specific structures, survive time limits). Enemy AI escalates aggression.
notable_spikes: First air unit mission (vertical axis introduces new threat vector)
First naval-only campaign segment (faction weakness exposed)
Late Soviet missions with Tesla coil defense grids (high-risk base layouts)
Final campaign missions combining all unit types in single engagement

notable_level_patterns.description: Each mission offers two distinct map layouts with identical objective but different strategic approaches. One route typically offers direct assault path; other offers flanking/resource-rich perimeter path. Allows player skill expression and replayability.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic across campaign (stated in Wikipedia as main design principle)

notable_level_patterns.description: Missions deliberately place players into unfavorable matchups (e.g., Soviet naval missions where submarines are only offensive option) forcing adaptation and teaching counter-play.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Soviet submarine-heavy maps; Allied missions with dense Tesla coil formations

notable_level_patterns.description: Early phases establish base and gather resources; mid-phase introduces secondary objectives (capture structures, defend positions); late-phase combines sustained base defense with active offensive operations.
Maps limit ore availability or force choices between expansion (more ore trucks, additional refineries) vs. military buildup. Creates resource scarcity tension.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-game missions with clustered ore fields and limited silo space
